title: Fideo Seco Dry Mexican Noodles
description: Fideo Seco is a deeply comforting and flavorful Mexican pasta dish, featuring toasted vermicelli noodles simmered in a rich, vibrant tomato and chili sauce until perfectly tender and dry. This beloved staple of Mexican home cooking offers a delightful balance of savory, subtly spicy, and tangy notes, making it a perfect side or light main course.

ingredients.item: Fideo noodles (vermicelli, thin spaghetti broken into 1-inch pieces)

ingredients.notes: for a subtle smoky heat, optional

instructions.title: Prepare the Tomato Sauce Base
instructions.text: Roughly chop the Roma tomatoes, 1/4 of the white onion, and peel the garlic cloves. If using, add the chipotle peppers (with a spoon of adobo sauce) to a blender. Combine these ingredients in the blender with 1/2 cup of the chicken broth. Blend until completely smooth. Set aside.

instructions.title: Toast the Fideo Noodles
instructions.text: Heat the vegetable oil or lard in a large, deep skillet or pot over medium-high heat. Add the fideo noodles (if using thin spaghetti, ensure it's broken into 1-inch pieces). Stir constantly to ensure even browning. Cook for about 3-5 minutes, or until the noodles turn a deep golden brown. Be careful not to burn them, as this will result in a bitter taste.

instructions.title: Simmer the Sauce
instructions.text: Carefully pour the blended tomato sauce into the skillet with the toasted fideo. The sauce will splatter, so be cautious. Stir well to coat all the noodles. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer, stirring occasionally for about 2 minutes to allow the flavors to meld and the sauce to reduce slightly.

instructions.title: Add Broth and Cook
instructions.text: Pour in the remaining 2.5 cups of chicken broth and add the salt. Stir everything together. Bring the mixture to a boil, then immediately reduce the heat to low. Cover the skillet tightly with a lid.

instructions.title: Absorb and Rest
instructions.text: Allow the fideo to simmer gently, covered, for 15-20 minutes, or until all the liquid has been absorbed and the noodles are tender. Avoid lifting the lid too often during this time. Once cooked, remove the skillet from the heat and let it rest, still covered, for 5 minutes. This allows the noodles to fully steam and prevents them from becoming mushy.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Fluff the fideo seco gently with a fork. Serve hot, garnished generously with fresh chopped cilantro and crumbled queso fresco or Cotija cheese. A drizzle of Crema Mexicana is an excellent optional addition for richness.

tips: **Even Browning is Key:** Don't rush the toasting of the fideo. Evenly golden-brown noodles develop a deeper, nuttier flavor that is crucial for this dish's authenticity and taste.
**Control the Heat:** Once the broth is added, reduce the heat to low and cover tightly. This ensures the noodles cook evenly by steaming and absorbing the liquid without burning at the bottom.
**Spice Adjustment:** For a milder flavor, omit the chipotle. For more heat, add an extra chipotle or a pinch of arbol chili flakes to the sauce.
**Resting Period:** The 5-minute resting period off the heat is essential. It allows the noodles to finish absorbing any remaining liquid and become perfectly tender without being soggy.
article:
## History of Fideo Seco Dry Mexican Noodles

Fideo, meaning "noodle" in Spanish, has a rich history in Mexican cuisine, tracing its roots back to the Spanish colonial era. Spanish settlers introduced pasta to Mexico, and over centuries, it was adapted to local ingredients and culinary traditions. Fideo seco, or "dry noodles," became a beloved staple, particularly in central Mexico. It's a testament to the ingenuity of Mexican cooks who transformed a European ingredient into something distinctly Mexican, often served as a humble yet satisfying family meal. It evokes feelings of nostalgia and home, a dish passed down through generations, embodying comfort and tradition.

## Why This Recipe Works

This Fideo Seco recipe works beautifully because it employs several fundamental Mexican cooking principles. The crucial step of toasting the fideo noodles in oil before adding the sauce imparts a deep, nutty flavor and a delightful texture that prevents them from becoming mushy. The fresh tomato-based sauce, blended with onion, garlic, and a hint of chipotle, provides a vibrant, balanced foundation. Simmering the noodles directly in this sauce allows them to absorb all the rich flavors, resulting in a cohesive dish where every strand of fideo is infused with deliciousness. It's a simple absorption method that yields maximum flavor and perfect noodle consistency.

## Chef Tips for Perfection

For truly perfect Fideo Seco, pay close attention to the browning of your noodles – they should be a rich, even golden brown, but never burnt. Use good quality chicken broth for the best flavor; homemade is ideal, but a low-sodium store-bought option works well. Ensure your blender sauce is perfectly smooth; any chunks of tomato or onion can alter the final texture. When simmering, resist the urge to constantly lift the lid, as this releases steam vital for cooking the noodles. Finally, don't skimp on the garnishes – fresh cilantro, crumbled queso fresco, and a dollop of crema Mexicana add layers of freshness, saltiness, and creaminess that elevate the dish significantly.

## Serving Suggestions

Fideo Seco is incredibly versatile. It can be served as a delightful side dish to a variety of main courses such as carne asada, pollo asado, or enchiladas. It also makes a satisfying light main course when accompanied by a simple green salad with a vinaigrette dressing. For a heartier meal, you can stir in cooked shredded chicken or ground beef directly into the fideo during the last few minutes of cooking. Don't forget fresh warm tortillas on the side for scooping up every last bit of the flavorful noodles. A side of sliced avocado also complements its rich flavors beautifully.

recipeIngredient: 7 oz Fideo noodles (vermicelli, thin spaghetti broken into 1-inch pieces) (approx. 200g, one standard package)
1-2 Chipotle in adobo sauce (for a subtle smoky heat, optional)
1/2 cup Queso fresco or Cotija cheese (crumbled, for garnish)
